product: Refine AI-Powered Product Search and Discovery Platform

system_description.overview: Refine Technology Inc provides AI-powered search and product discovery tools for e-commerce, specializing in fashion and luxury retail. The platform delivers multimodal search (text + image), product recommendations, and behavioral analytics via a hosted API that integrates with Shopify and custom storefronts. Services are designed to drive conversion rates and automate merchandising workflows.

products.AI-Powered Product Search and Discovery: Multimodal search engine (text, image, hybrid) with real-time ranking algorithms adapting to customer behavior
products.Product Recommendation Engine: Personalized suggestions based on browsing patterns, cart behavior, user profiles, and campaign customization
products.Merchandising and Analytics Tools: Real-time dashboards for search performance, zero-result queries, conversion attribution, and behavioral analytics
products.Hosted API & Infrastructure: Fully hosted managed API service supporting enterprise-grade availability via secure endpoints
products.Support and Implementation Services: White-glove onboarding, dedicated account management, and technical solutions engineering

infrastructure.cloud_provider: GCP (carve-out — GCP controls excluded from scope)
regions: Not explicitly named; GCP multi-AZ described
infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices or servers; fully cloud-hosted on GCP
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (multiple servers spun up across availability zones on AZ failure)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Hosted in GCP VPC environment with segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs). Customer accounts are segmented from each other (CC6.1.3). System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network.
network_architecture.api_gateway: API Gateway (shown in network diagram as entry point from Client Applications)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS employed via GCP to identify and protect against threats
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host with VPN for remote access
network_architecture.tls: TLS (Transport Layer Security) with certificates from established third-party CA for all user requests
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; endpoints protected with antivirus and email scanning
network_architecture.topology: Client Applications → API Gateway → Authentication Service → microservices (Catalog Service, Search Service, Events Service) → Pub/Sub → downstream services (Image Downloader, Catalog AI, Analytics API, Analytics Ingest, Recommendations Service, Billing Cron) → data stores (PostgreSQL, Cloud Storage, Pinecone, ClickHouse, Redis) + Stripe + Retool. Search AI communicates via gRPC.

application_architecture.api_style: REST + gRPC (gRPC shown for Search AI in network diagram)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments (CC8.1.4 — changes tested in dev/test before production)

data_storage.backup_frequency: Backup procedures defined in Backup Policy; frequency not explicitly stated beyond 'suitable backups taken'

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: GCP (passwords controlled through GCP Password policy; Active Directory / server / database accounts mentioned)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform (CC6.1.1). Source code tool access requires MFA (CC8.1.3). Email requires 2FA.
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: GCP password policy with periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements, and account lockout after failed attempts

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider KMS (CC6.1.5 — organization uses cloud provider key management service to encrypt data at rest)
encryption.key_management: GCP KMS; access to production encryption keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named explicitly (referred to as 'source code management tool' with MFA-protected access and branch protection rules)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to require additional approval for every merge request to production (CC8.1.2)
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline configurations, implementation, and rollback (CC7.1.1) — specific tool not named
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO sets up automatic patch management with monthly reviews; workstations receive automatic updates; OS-level vulnerability scanning monthly

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (described as 'monitoring tools' for capacity and anomaly detection)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not named (logs enabled at application and infrastructure level)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured for high/critical risk events; IT management notified via incident management process

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure — compute, storage, networking, KMS

security_tools.antivirus: Endpoint security solution on all desktops and laptops; anti-virus/malware on all production servers with continuous auto-scanning; AV definitions updated weekly on servers, daily on workstations
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications (CC7.1.2); self-assessment scan prior to every production release
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party (CC4.1.1)

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical access to data center facilities; GCP responsible for physical security
A1.2 — Environmental protections/backup processes; Refine has no physical office, managed by building/GCP
Processing Integrity trust criteria — determined not relevant to system
Privacy trust criteria — determined not relevant to system
notable_controls: CC8.1.2 — Branch protection rules require additional approval for every merge request to production
CC8.1.3 — Source code management tool access requires MFA
CC6.2.2 — Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged
CC6.1.4 — Access revoked within one business day of termination
CC7.1.2 — Monthly automated vulnerability scans with high/critical remediation tracking
CC4.1.1 — Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
CC7.4.1 — BCDR tested annually across multiple loss scenarios
CC9.2.1 — Annual SOC report reviews of critical vendors; quarterly risk assessments for vendors without SOC reports

infrastructure.rationale: GCP-hosted with multi-AZ failover, VPC segmentation, IDS/IPS, VPN bastion. No multi-region, no CDN, no named IaC tool. Pinecone and ClickHouse are purpose-fit data stores showing architectural maturity. Region names not disclosed.

application_architecture.rationale: Clear microservices architecture with distinct services (Catalog, Search, Events, Recommendations, Analytics), gRPC for internal AI calls, separate environments, and branch protection. Languages and frameworks not named, container orchestration not mentioned.

data_layer.rationale: Diverse, purpose-fit data stores: PostgreSQL (transactional), ClickHouse (analytics), Pinecone (vector/AI), Redis (cache), GCP Cloud Storage (objects). Multi-AZ, per-tenant segregation, data classification and retention policies in place. Backup frequency not specified precisely.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, IDS/IPS, firewall, MFA on admin/production/SCM, quarterly access reviews, antivirus on all endpoints, log protection, vendor management. No WAF named, no SIEM named, no cybersecurity insurance mentioned.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection with required approvals, separate environments, emergency change process, IaC (config management tool unnamed), monthly patch management. CI/CD toolchain, source control system, and container orchestration all unnamed — limits score.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, reviewed and tested annually across multiple loss scenarios, multi-AZ failover documented. No RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region failover, daily backup frequency not confirmed.

vendor_diversity.rationale: 5 named vendors (GCP, Stripe, Retool, Pinecone, ClickHouse). GCP is a single critical dependency for compute/network/KMS. Pinecone is a critical single-vendor for the core AI search function. Good diversity for analytics and billing layers.

overall.rationale: Refine shows a well-architected microservices platform on GCP with purpose-built data stores (Pinecone + ClickHouse is notably mature for a Type 1 report). Security controls are solid. Key gaps: no CI/CD toolchain named, no WAF, no SIEM, RTO/RPO not disclosed, single critical dependency on Pinecone for core search function.

red_flags: No WAF explicitly deployed or named
No SIEM or named log aggregation platform
CI/CD tool and source control system names not disclosed — limits pipeline auditability
No multi-region failover (only multi-AZ within GCP)
yellow_flags: Backup frequency not explicitly stated (policy exists but interval unclear)
Container orchestration not mentioned — unclear if services are containerized
Pinecone is a critical single-vendor dependency for core AI search capability
Programming languages and frameworks not disclosed
green_flags: Unusually mature data architecture for a Type 1 company: PostgreSQL + ClickHouse + Pinecone + Redis shows deliberate purpose-fit stack
Network diagram present in report showing full microservices topology — rare level of transparency
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with high/critical remediation tracking
Quarterly user access reviews covering all accounts including privileged
Access revocation within one business day
MFA enforced at admin access, production platform, and source code management
Branch protection with required approval for every production merge
BCDR tested annually across multiple loss scenarios
Multi-AZ failover with GCP availability zone spanning
key_observations: Pinecone as vector DB is a strong signal of AI-native architecture — this is not a bolted-on ML layer; it is core to the search product
ClickHouse for analytics (rather than BigQuery or Redshift) suggests performance-optimized analytics engineering awareness
The network diagram reveals gRPC for Search AI communication, indicating a deliberate performance-oriented internal API design
Retool as an internal tool suggests the team relies on low-code for internal operations — common at this stage but worth noting for scalability
The Accorp Partners auditor is a common provider for early-stage SOC 2 Type 1 reports — not a Big 4 or specialist firm
Privacy and Processing Integrity criteria explicitly scoped out — appropriate for a B2B API but limits future enterprise customer trust use cases
No physical presence whatsoever — fully cloud-native, which reduces physical security risk but increases cloud vendor concentration risk
